demonstrated: Same-toolchain builds remain byte-identical in the unit test.
New snapshot metadata records Python zipfile/zlib scope and runtime versions.
Documentation now identifies Git-tree and payload-manifest identity as the cross-toolchain invariant.
AUDOBS-0010.implemented: Universal outer-ZIP byte identity is no longer claimed; exact frozen artifact hashes remain preserved and rebuild payload identity remains bound to the immutable Git tree.
AUDOBS-0010.limitations: No second compression toolchain was executed by this change. Cross-toolchain compressed-byte equality is explicitly a non-claim.

demonstrated: A mocked execution test asserts repository creation omits --push.
The test asserts visibility verification and the mandatory permission request occur before git push.
A PUBLIC response stops before permission configuration or push.
AUDOBS-0014.implemented: github_bootstrap creates an empty private remote, verifies GitHub reports PRIVATE, configures mandatory Actions permissions, and only then pushes content.
AUDOBS-0014.limitations: Tests use an isolated in-process mock and do not prove live GitHub provider semantics.

demonstrated: A simulated Actions-permission failure writes a FAILED receipt, raises NexusError and performs no content push.
Simulated label and issue failures return PARTIAL with explicit warning objects.
AUDOBS-0015.implemented: Mandatory and optional bootstrap failures now have different explicit policies instead of being silently absorbed as raw exit codes.
AUDOBS-0015.limitations: A successful CLI exit code is not independent provider-state attestation.

demonstrated: Unit tests exercise BLOCK, WARN, ADVISORY and legacy-untyped behavior.
The CLI invokes assurance evaluation before dispatch.
Status and diagnostic commands remain available under a wildcard block.
AUDOBS-0016.implemented: STATUS.json supports typed command-scoped assurance gates enforced by the Nexus CLI.
AUDOBS-0016.limitations: Direct shell commands, manual file edits and external tools can bypass the Nexus CLI gate.

non_claims: A passing workflow does not establish complete correctness, security, privacy or scientific validity.
Mocked GitHub bootstrap tests do not create a real repository or prove provider-side confidentiality.
This receipt does not alter baseline-001, canonical snapshots, R001/R002 targets, audit ledgers or preserved evidence.
Final promotion still requires the pull-request head, including this receipt and final status files, to pass the Nexus Audit workflow.

preserved_without_retroactive_ledger_mut.reason: The observations and bounded adjudication reports are preserved with status_authority NONE. The closeout records user-authorized remediation and does not rewrite history or convert reports into self-authorizing ledger entries.

properties_exercised: full unit and attack test suite
